_brand.mission: Unlock limitless potential and lead the future with innovative technologies.
_brand.tagline: Powered by People, Driven by Innovation.

_logo.placement: bottom-right, seated on a light rounded chip (#f5f1f5 at ~92% opacity) for contrast against the dark background
never: Do not place a second logo or add 'EvolveX' text elsewhere in the generated content
Do not remove or cover the light chip behind the logo

_color_palette.note: All colors are lifted directly from the EvolveX logo gradient — do not substitute unrelated brand colors (e.g. teal, blue, cyan).

A.name: Bold Text + Person
A.when_to_use: Personal/inspirational posts, culture or team-driven stories, hiring/career content.
A.description: Large title text dominating the upper two-thirds, warm orange glow on the headline, with a photorealistic confident professional (male or female) positioned to one side against the dark plum gradient. Subtle magenta rim-light on the subject.
A.accent_usage: Orange for headline glow, magenta for rim lighting, gold used sparingly for a single highlight accent (e.g. a stat badge).

B.name: Tech / Automation Infographic
B.when_to_use: RPA workflow explainers, agentic AI systems, multi-step automation pipelines, 'how it works' posts.
B.description: Connected hexagonal or circular nodes representing workflow steps (e.g. Trigger → Extract → Validate → Act), joined by thin gradient connector lines running orange-to-magenta. Each node has a dark plum interior with a thin pink/purple border glow and a short white label.
B.accent_usage: Magenta/purple for node borders and connectors, orange for the primary flow direction or the 'result' node, gold for a single standout metric (e.g. '75% faster').

C.name: Split Layout
C.when_to_use: Contrast or comparison posts (e.g. AUTOMATE vs KEEP HUMAN, before/after, manual vs automated process).
C.description: Two-column layout with a soft vertical divider down the center. Left column typically the 'before' or 'human' side in cooler purple tones; right column the 'after' or 'automated' side in warmer orange tones, echoing the logo's own left-to-right gradient sweep.
C.accent_usage: Purple dominant on the left column, orange dominant on the right column, magenta as the divider/connector color.

_scene_background_rule.gradient: Near-black plum (#0b0710) in the upper-left, fading through deep magenta-plum (#2a0f30), to a warm orange glow (#F1600A) in the lower-right corner.
_scene_background_rule.critical: The inner generated content's background MUST match this gradient so the seam against the template frame is invisible. Never use teal, blue, black, or flat white/light backgrounds.
